generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance ForceActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Autonomous actuator system that adjusts physical parameters of the Physical Twin (PT) based on Digital Twin (DT) optimization recommendations, such as recalibrating control systems or rerouting workflows."
]

instance EnvironmentalController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cyber-physical controller that manages environmental interactions with the PT, implementing adjustments suggested by the DT's predictive models to optimize performance under varying conditions."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OperationalForceSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision sensor measuring dynamic forces acting on the Physical Twin (PT) during operation, providing real-time telemetry for model calibration and anomaly detection."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalForceData
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Multi-parametric sensor suite capturing environmental interactions (e.g., temperature gradients, aerodynamic forces) affecting the PT's performance."
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalConditionData
]

instance StructuralHealthMonitor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Embedded sensor network monitoring structural integrity (e.g., stress distribution, vibration patterns) of the PT to validate physics-based models against real-world conditions."
    DTDFVocab:producedData StructuralHealthData
]

instance OperationalParameterSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time sensor capturing operational parameters (e.g., motion trajectories, energy consumption) to enable predictive analytics and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:producedData OperationalParameterData
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OperationalForceData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalForceSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalConditionSensor
]

instance StructuralHealthData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom StructuralHealthMonitor
]

instance OperationalParameterData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OperationalParameterSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance GeometricModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Abstract CAD-based representation of the Physical Twin's (PT) structural and spatial properties, serving as the foundational geometric input for behavioral simulations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo GeometricModelingEngine
]

instance BehavioralModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Dynamic simulation model using differential equations to predict PT behavior under forces, environmental conditions, and operational constraints."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulationEngine
    DTDFVocab:inputTo KalmanFilterEstimator
]

instance PhysicsBasedFaultDetector : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based analytical model employing state estimators to detect anomalies in PT behavior by comparing real-time sensor data against expected dynamic responses."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo FaultDetectionEngine
]

instance DataDrivenAnomalyDetector : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Machine learning-based model (e.g., autoencoder) trained on historical PT data to identify deviations from normal operational patterns."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MachineLearningEngine
]

instance OptimizationAlgorithm : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Multi-objective optimization framework (e.g., genetic algorithm) that suggests PT parameter adjustments to maximize efficiency or minimize costs."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo OptimizationEngine
]

instance SensedDataRepository : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series database storing real-time sensor data streams from the PT for model calibration and historical analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalForceData, EnvironmentalConditionData, StructuralHealthData, OperationalParameterData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingPipeline
]

instance HistoricalPerformanceData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Archived operational logs and trend data used for retrospective validation of DT models and benchmarking against current PT performance."
    DTDFVocab:fromData OperationalParameterData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ValidationEngine
]

// C11: Enablers
instance GeometricModelingEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "CAD and geometric modeling framework enabling the creation and manipulation of abstract representations of the PT's physical structure."
    DTDFVocab:enables GeometricModelingService
]

instance SimulationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Physics-based simulation solver that executes behavioral models to predict PT dynamics under real-time or hypothetical conditions."
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveSimulationService
]

instance KalmanFilterEstimator : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "State estimation algorithm processing sensor data at sub-second intervals to refine model predictions and detect deviations."
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService
]

instance FaultDetectionEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Analytical engine combining physics-based and data-driven models to identify PT anomalies and validate model accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:enables AnomalyDetectionService
]

instance MachineLearningEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Machine learning platform supporting unsupervised and supervised learning techniques for pattern recognition and predictive analytics."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataDrivenInsightService
]

instance OptimizationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Algorithmic framework implementing multi-objective optimization techniques to suggest PT parameter adjustments."
    DTDFVocab:enables PerformanceOptimizationService
]

instance DataProcessingPipeline : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time data processing middleware normalizing, filtering, and aggregating sensor streams for model integration."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataIntegrationService
]

instance ValidationEngine : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model validation platform comparing DT predictions against ground-truth data (e.g., lab tests, historical records) to assess accuracy."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelValidationService
]

instance VisualizationDashboard : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization platform overlaying DT-generated data (e.g., thermal maps, stress distributions) onto PT representations."
    DTDFVocab:enables VisualAnalyticsService
]

// C6: Services
instance GeometricModelingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing abstract geometric representations of the PT for use in behavioral simulations and optimization."
    DTDFVocab:provides GeometricModelInsight
]

instance PredictiveSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service simulating PT behavior under current or hypothetical conditions to enable what-if analysis and trajectory forecasting."
    DTDFVocab:provides BehaviorPredictionInsight
]

instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service continuously assessing PT health and operational parameters via real-time sensor data processing."
    DTDFVocab:provides HealthMonitoringInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyAlertAction
]

instance AnomalyDetectionService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service identifying PT anomalies through hybrid physics-based and data-driven models, flagging deviations from expected behavior."
    DTDFVocab:provides AnomalyDetectionInsight
]

instance DataDrivenInsightService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service generating predictive and causal insights using machine learning techniques applied to historical and real-time PT data."
    DTDFVocab:provides PredictiveInsight
    DTDFVocab:provides RootCauseAnalysisInsight
]

instance PerformanceOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service applying optimization algorithms to suggest PT parameter adjustments for improved efficiency or safety."
    DTDFVocab:provides OptimizationRecommendationAction
]

instance DataIntegrationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service managing data ingestion, preprocessing, and integration between PT sensor streams and DT models."
    DTDFVocab:provides DataQualityInsight
]

instance ModelValidationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service validating DT models against ground-truth data to ensure predictive accuracy and reliability."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelAccuracyInsight
]

instance VisualAnalyticsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Service providing interactive visualizations of DT insights, enabling intuitive exploration of PT state and performance."
    DTDFVocab:provides VisualizationInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance GeometricModelInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Abstract geometric representation of the PT's structural properties, used as foundational input for behavioral simulations."
]

instance BehaviorPredictionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predicted dynamic behavior of the PT under current or hypothetical conditions, enabling what-if analysis and failure mode simulation."
]

instance HealthMonitoringInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous assessment of PT operational parameters and environmental interactions, providing real-time health status updates."
]

instance AnomalyAlertAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated alert system flagging detected anomalies in PT behavior based on deviations from expected model outputs."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance AnomalyDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identified deviations from normal PT operational patterns, categorized by severity and potential root causes."
]

instance PredictiveInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Time-series forecasts of PT behavior (e.g., failure modes, energy consumption trends) generated using advanced analytics."
]

instance RootCauseAnalysisInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Causal relationships between PT parameters and observed deviations, identified using techniques like Granger causality or Bayesian networks."
]

instance OptimizationRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggested adjustments to PT parameters (e.g., controller settings, maintenance schedules) to maximize efficiency or minimize costs."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance DataQualityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Assessment of sensor data accuracy and consistency, ensuring reliable input for model calibration and validation."
]

instance ModelAccuracyInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Quantitative measure of DT model predictive accuracy, validated against ground-truth data and historical performance benchmarks."
]

instance VisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D representations and dashboards overlaying DT-generated data onto PT visualizations for intuitive analysis."
]

// C12: Environment
instance OperationalEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Dynamic environment encompassing physical forces, environmental interactions, and operational constraints affecting the PT."
    base:contains OperationalForceSensor, EnvironmentalConditionSensor, StructuralHealthMonitor, OperationalParameterSensor
]

instance DigitalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Cyber environment hosting all DT models, algorithms, and services that interact with the Physical Twin (PT)."
    base:contains GeometricModelingEngine, SimulationEngine, KalmanFilterEstimator, FaultDetectionEngine, MachineLearningEngine, OptimizationEngine, DataProcessingPipeline, ValidationEngine, VisualizationDashboard
]

// C13: Components
instance PhysicalTwin : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Cyber-Physical System (CPS) representing the real-world entity being digitally twinned, characterized by dynamic interactions between physical forces and cyber elements."
    base:isContainedIn OperationalEnvironment
]

instance DigitalTwinCore : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Core computational framework integrating all DT models, data processing pipelines, and algorithmic enablers to enable real-time analysis and decision support."
    base:isContainedIn DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance DigitalTwinSystem : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "Digital Twin system representing the complete cyber-physical framework for monitoring and optimizing the Physical Twin."
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment DigitalTwinSystem
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
    - **Control Flows from Digital to Physical**:
      - **Command Types**: Actions derived from DT simulations or algorithms, such as adjustments to system parameters, fault mitigation commands, or optimization directives (e.g., reconfiguring control systems).
      - **Control Message Formats**: Not explicitly specified, but likely structured messages (e.g., JSON, XML, or protocol-specific payloads) to ensure compatibility with the PT's control interfaces.
      - **Validation and Authentication Mechanisms**: Not detailed, but implied to exist to prevent unauthorized or erroneous commands (e.g., digital signatures, access controls, or protocol-level validation).
      - **Feedback Systems**: The PT provides feedback (e.g., status updates, sensor data) to the DT to validate command execution and adjust models/algorithms dynamically.
      - **Conflict Resolution**: Not explicitly described, but likely handled via supervisory controllers or human intervention when violations (e.g., safety constraints) are detected.
      - **Command Execution Confirmation**: The DT may confirm receipt and execution of commands via acknowledgment mechanisms (e.g., return codes, status flags) from the PT.

    - **Key Processes**:
      The DT generates commands (e.g., via optimizers or controllers) and sends them to the PT. Human or algorithmic oversight resolves conflicts, and feedback loops ensure alignment between digital and physical states.
  "
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system operates with a temporal synchronization framework that integrates real-time data streams from the Physical Twin (PT) via sensors, enabling continuous calibration and monitoring. The system employs **calibration algorithms** to update models dynamically with the best parameter estimates, ensuring temporal alignment between the digital and physical components. While explicit synchronization frequencies (e.g., Hz or sampling rates) are not detailed, the system implies **low-latency requirements** for monitoring and fault detection, as violations are addressed promptly via human or algorithmic intervention. Temporal granularity is implicitly defined by the **operational conditions of the PT**, where models must reflect real-time dynamics (e.g., differential equations for force-movement analysis) to support predictive simulations and optimizations. The system emphasizes **real-time or near-real-time** data processing to maintain coherence between the digital and physical domains, though no specific latency thresholds (e.g., <100ms) or granularity constraints (e.g., sub-second updates) are explicitly quantified in the provided context."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **modular architecture pattern** that integrates three core components: **data** (collected via sensors from the PT), **models** (abstract representations of the PT’s physics and cyber aspects), and **algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators, and optimizers). These components are orchestrated through a **feedback loop**: sensor data informs calibration algorithms, which update models; updated models feed into monitors for safety/optimality checks; and violations trigger corrective actions (human or algorithmic). The system employs **real-time coordination** via algorithmic decision-making to maintain alignment between the digital and physical domains. Resource management is implied through the **dynamic updating of models** based on real-time data, ensuring adaptive behavior. No explicit mention of **orchestration frameworks** (e.g., Kubernetes, microservices) or **standardized integration patterns** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) is provided."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The lifecycle of a Digital Twin (DT) spans multiple stages, beginning with **model development**—where abstract representations (e.g., CAD models for geometric dimensions or dynamic models for physics-based simulations) are constructed to analyze specific system aspects (e.g., forces on a car chassis or differential equations for movement). These models are not discarded post-deployment but **integrated into the DT framework** to enable continuous monitoring and optimization.

Key phases include:
1. **Design Phase**: Creation of **aspect-specific models** (e.g., CAD for geometry, physics-based models for dynamics) to understand system behavior, optimize performance, and communicate among stakeholders. These models are derived from domain-specific knowledge (e.g., differential equations for mechanical systems).
2. **Deployment Phase**: The DT is deployed alongside the Physical Twin (PT), where it **integrates real-time data** from sensors (e.g., force sensors, motion trackers) to update its internal models via **calibration algorithms**. These algorithms refine parameters (e.g., mass distribution, friction coefficients) to align the DT’s predictive models with observed PT behavior.
3. **Operational Phase**: The DT employs **monitoring algorithms** (e.g., fault detectors, state estimators) to assess PT performance against safety and optimality criteria. Violations trigger **decision-making processes**, where human operators or automated controllers adjust the PT’s operating conditions (e.g., recalibrating control systems, reconfiguring workflows).
4. **Evolution Phase**: The DT undergoes **continuous updates** via feedback loops, incorporating new sensor data, model refinements, and algorithmic improvements. This ensures the DT’s predictive accuracy and decision-support capabilities remain aligned with evolving PT dynamics.

Lifecycle governance involves **seamless transition management** between phases, ensuring models, data, and algorithms are consistently updated and validated. The DT’s lifecycle is governed by iterative **model-data-algorithm integration**, where each component (data from sensors, models of physics/cyber systems, and algorithms for analysis/control) is dynamically synchronized to maintain the DT’s relevance and utility.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin system emphasizes **fidelity and validity** through structured methodologies to ensure accuracy and reliability:
- **Model Fidelity Requirements**:
  - Models must capture critical aspects of the PT’s cyber-physical system (CPS) and environmental interactions, including dynamic behaviors (e.g., force-response relationships) and operational constraints.
  - **Uncertainty Sources**: Quantified via calibration algorithms that account for sensor noise, environmental variability, and model parameter deviations.
- **Validation and Verification Methods**:
  - **Calibration Algorithms**: Continuously update model parameters (e.g., CPS/environment parameters) using real-time sensed data to minimize prediction errors.
  - **Simulation-Data Alignment**: Behavioral models are validated against PT data streams to ensure consistency in dynamic predictions.
  - **Fault Detection**: Monitors analyze deviations between DT predictions and PT observations, triggering alerts for anomalies or performance violations.
- **Error Detection and Correction Mechanisms**:
  - **State Estimators**: Use sensed data to refine model states, reducing drift in long-term predictions.
  - **Supervisory Controllers**: Automatically adjust PT operations (e.g., actuator settings) if violations are detected, ensuring adherence to safety constraints.
- **Quality Assurance Processes**:
  - **Continuous Monitoring**: Real-time feedback loops validate model integrity against PT performance metrics.
  - **Retrospective Analysis**: Historical data is used to iteratively improve model fidelity and calibration techniques.
  - **Human-in-the-Loop**: Engineers intervene to validate critical decisions (e.g., adjusting models or PT configurations) based on monitored violations or unexpected behaviors.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
    - **Technical Network Infrastructure**:
      - **Communication Protocols**: Not explicitly named, but standard industrial protocols (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or proprietary) are implied for bidirectional PT-DT communication.
      - **Network Topology and Architecture**: Likely a hybrid architecture connecting edge devices (sensors/actuators) to central DT platforms (cloud/edge/on-premise), with potential mesh or star topologies for reliability.
      - **Bandwidth and Latency Requirements**: Not specified, but critical for real-time applications (e.g., low-latency for control loops, high bandwidth for telemetry).
      - **Reliability and Redundancy Mechanisms**: Not detailed, but implied via continuous monitoring and calibration algorithms to handle data loss or failures.
      - **Security Protocols**: Not mentioned, but essential for protecting data integrity (e.g., encryption, authentication, and secure APIs for DT access).
  "
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
    - **Deployment Infrastructure**:
      - **Hosting Platforms**: Supports **cloud**, **edge**, or **on-premise** deployments, depending on scalability and latency needs (e.g., edge for low-latency control, cloud for centralized analytics).
      - **Computational and Storage Requirements**: High computational power for simulations/models and scalable storage for historical data, sensor logs, and DT assets.
      - **Scalability Mechanisms**: Not explicitly described, but implied via integration of models/algorithms that adapt to system complexity (e.g., modular DT components).
      - **Deployment Models Supported**: Likely supports hybrid models (e.g., edge for real-time processing + cloud for analytics) and possibly containerized or microservices architectures.
      - **Availability/Reliability Assurance**: Ensured via continuous monitoring, redundancy in data/processing, and integration with physical system feedback loops.
  "
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system’s horizontal integration is not explicitly detailed in the provided context. While it references **data exchange** from the PT via sensors and **model integration** for simulation/optimization, no specific **interoperability standards** (e.g., ISO/IEC 42010, OMG’s Digital Twin Reference Architecture), **data exchange protocols** (e.g., REST, SOAP, DDS), or **dependencies on external systems** (e.g., ERP, MES) are mentioned. The system’s scope is primarily confined to the **DT-PT relationship**, without addressing integration with other Digital Twins or enterprise systems. ]

    base:desc "

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) framework integrates **cybersecurity protocols** and **access control mechanisms** to ensure the integrity and confidentiality of data exchanged between the Physical Twin (PT) and the DT. **Authentication systems** are employed to validate the identity of users or systems interacting with the DT, preventing unauthorized access to critical models and algorithms. **Threat mitigation strategies** include real-time monitoring of system performance via **fault detectors** and **state estimators**, which identify deviations from expected behavior, enabling timely intervention. **Fail-safe mechanisms** are embedded within the DT to automatically enforce corrective actions when violations (e.g., safety or operational thresholds) are detected, as described in the calibration and monitoring workflows. **Risk assessment approaches** are implicitly supported by the DT’s ability to simulate and predict PT behavior under varying conditions, allowing proactive identification of potential risks. For **safety-critical systems**, the DT employs **supervisory controllers** and **optimizers** to enforce constraints and ensure compliance with operational safety protocols, leveraging real-time data from the PT to adjust models dynamically. The framework also relies on **calibration algorithms** to refine system parameters, ensuring that safety-critical decisions are based on the most accurate and up-to-date representations of the PT’s physical and cyber-physical states."
]
